channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Câmara trava na volta do recesso e fica sem votações em quase metade dos dias úteis
item.description: The Chamber of Deputies held votes on only 21 of 39 business days between February 2 and the end of March 2026, reflecting a recurring pattern of low legislative productivity observed since 2023, regardless of the chamber's presidency. This slowdown is influenced by Carnival, the entrenched use of semi-remote sessions, and the proximity of the 2026 presidential elections, although significant bills such as the Anti-Faction Bill and the Public Security PEC were approved.

item.title: Caiado mira em Lula ao assumir candidatura e testa discurso para não ser visto como 'linha auxiliar' de Flávio
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado has been confirmed as the PSD's presidential candidate for 2026, aiming to position himself as an independent alternative by directly confronting Lula while avoiding alignment with Flávio Bolsonaro. His strategy centers on criticizing Lula's government on public safety and the economy, leveraging his record in Goiás, while rejecting bolsonarist ties to appeal to centrist and moderate right-wing voters.

item.title: STF julga eleição do Rio dia 8, e PL pede que eleito na Alerj assuma governo
item.description: The Brazilian Supreme Court (STF) will rule on April 8 on whether Rio de Janeiro will hold a direct or indirect election for an interim governor to serve until year-end. The PL party has requested amicus curiae status, arguing that the newly elected president of the State Legislative Assembly (Alerj) should automatically assume the interim governorship under the traditional succession line, regardless of the STF’s final decision on the election model. The move aims to enable Douglas Ruas, PL’s pre-candidate and favorite to lead Alerj, to become interim governor before the interim election takes place.

item.title: Caiado ataca PT, critica falta de 'experiência' de Flávio Bolsonaro e promete anistia para tentar virar voto
item.description: Governor Ronaldo Caiado of Goiás was confirmed as the PSD's pre-candidate for the 2026 presidency, promising to deactivate national polarization. In his first speech, he criticized the PT and questioned Flávio Bolsonaro's experience for inter-branch dialogue, while announcing he would grant amnesty to those convicted in the January 8 events, including former president Jair Bolsonaro, as a pacification measure.

item.title: ACM Neto define vice e lança chapa para disputar o governo da Bahia
item.description: ACM Neto, the União Brasil pre-candidate for governor of Bahia, officially launched his 2026 election ticket at the CDL theater in Feira de Santana. The slate includes Jequié mayor Zé Cocá (PP) as vice-governor and senatorial candidates João Roma (PL) and Angelo Coronel (Republicanos).

item.title: Governo deve publicar MP para conter alta do diesel mesmo sem adesão total dos estados; veja os pontos
item.description: The Lula government plans to issue a provisional measure to subsidize imported diesel at R$ 1.20 per liter, with costs split equally between the Union and states, for two months, even without full state adherence. The estimated fiscal impact is R$ 1.5 billion per month. São Paulo and Sergipe have confirmed adherence, while other states are negotiating, with expectations of an 'expressive majority'.

item.title: Vibra reajusta em 54% querosene de aviação por alta do petróleo
item.description: Vibra Energia announced a 54.63% increase in aviation kerosene prices, effective April 1, 2026, driven by rising oil prices due to geopolitical tensions, including U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran that disrupted the Strait of Hormuz. The move comes amid criticism from President Lula’s government over the 2021 privatization of BR Distribuidora, with the PT pushing to reestablish state control through a parliamentary front. Although the PT aimed to launch the initiative by March 24, it failed to gather sufficient support.

item.title: Erika Hilton diz que sua eleição para Comissão foi contaminada por ‘discurso odioso’
item.description: Federal deputy Erika Hilton (PSOL-SP) denounced that her election as president of the Chamber’s Commission for the Defense of Women’s Rights was contaminated by hateful discourse that questioned her ability to represent women due to her transgender identity. She argued the controversy distracted from the Commission’s real priorities and criticized judicial institutions for failing to enforce the STF’s ruling equating transfobia with racism as a crime.

item.title: Moraes marca interrogatório de Eduardo Bolsonaro em ação por obstrução de Justiça e coação
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes has scheduled Eduardo Bolsonaro's interrogation for April 14 at 2:00 p.m. via videoconference in a case for obstruction of justice and coercion. The Prosecutor General's Office accuses him of repeatedly interfering in judicial proceedings involving his father, Jair Bolsonaro, while operating from the United States, allegedly coordinating with Paulo Figueiredo to pressure Brazilian authorities and influence court decisions, including efforts to trigger international sanctions and economic measures.

item.title: Moraes marca audiência de Eduardo para esclarecer sanções dos EUA ao Brasil
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes scheduled Eduardo Bolsonaro’s (PL-SP) interrogation for April 14 in a case where he is accused of obstruction of justice and coercion. The hearing aims to clarify U.S. sanctions imposed on Brazil related to the allegations against him.

item.title: Metade dos ministros deixa o governo, e Lula cobrará atuação em campanha eleitoral
item.description: President Lula convened his ministerial team to reinforce the need to defend government actions, particularly among ministers who will leave office to run in the October 2026 elections. The directive was aimed at encouraging these ministers to actively campaign in favor of the Workers' Party (PT).

item.title: Aliados de Castro veem intervenção do STF no RJ com manutenção de governador interino
item.description: Allies of former Governor Cláudio Castro (PL) view the STF's decision to maintain Justice Ricardo Couto as interim governor of Rio de Janeiro as a form of judicial intervention in the state. They argue that Couto's continued tenure until the election for the Guanabara Palace interferes with popular choice and represents an overreach of power. The measure was taken by the STF to ensure continuity of state administration until an elected governor is in place.

item.title: Caiado lança vídeo direcionado a mulheres e diz que tem mão pesada contra agressores
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, a PSD presidential pre-candidate, will release videos after Easter focusing on public security, including one targeting women on combating femicide. He emphasizes a 'heavy hand against aggressors,' reinforcing security as his main political banner.

item.title: Presidenciável, Caiado reedita tentativa de 1989 sob desafio de conquistar direita
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, 76, chosen by the PSD as its 2026 presidential candidate, states that his life trajectory aligns with the same electorate as former President Jair Bolsonaro, positioning himself as an alternative for voters rejecting both Lula and the Bolsonaro project now led by Flávio Bolsonaro. He aims to attract conservative voters but faces the challenge of differentiating himself from Bolsonaro while capturing his base.

item.title: Governo discute fundo para facilitar negociação de débitos de superendividados
item.description: The Lula government is discussing the creation of a fund to help over-indebted families negotiate their debts, as excessive income commitment to debt payments leaves them unable to cover basic expenses. The initiative aims to alleviate a growing social and economic crisis affecting millions of Brazilians.

item.title: Valdemar diz que Bolsonaro teria sido reeleito se apoiasse a vacinação
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, national president of the PL party, agreed with João Doria that Jair Bolsonaro would have been re-elected in 2022 had he supported vaccination and adopted a correct public health conduct during the pandemic. Doria stated that Bolsonaro’s mishandling of the health crisis cost him re-election, but emphasized that the focus should now be on Brazil’s future. The remarks were made at a Lide event in São Paulo, with Valdemar nodding in agreement beside Doria.

item.title: Moraes marca data de interrogatório de Eduardo Bolsonaro em ação penal
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes has scheduled Eduardo Bolsonaro’s (PL-SP) interrogation for April 14 in a criminal case where he is accused of obstruction of justice and coercion. The hearing will be held via videoconference, regardless of his participation, with the presence of the Prosecutor General’s Office and Federal Public Defender. The PGR alleges Eduardo prioritized personal and familial interests over the Republic’s, including efforts in the U.S. to impose sanctions and tariffs on Brazil.

item.title: Gramática prova Flávio golpista. Aberração de Caiado. Leite e o rabino
item.description: The article criticizes Flávio Bolsonaro for his allegedly coupist discourse, using grammatical analysis to argue that his conditional statement about victory — 'if our people can express themselves freely and votes are counted correctly' — reveals a mindset that only accepts electoral outcomes if favorable, making him a coupist even in the hypothetical case of defeat. It also mentions Ronaldo Caiado and Eduardo Leite as figures who, despite opposing Bolsonaro, reinforce a hollow and useless polarization, likened to the 'Bathroom Blonde' and the 'Sack Man' — myths that, though false, are deemed necessary to sustain the political game.

item.title: Free flow: Lula quer suspender multas e pedágio ganha viés político
item.description: The Lula government is studying the suspension of fines and license points for drivers who did not pay free-flow tolls in São Paulo, allowing payment until December 30, 2026. The measure is seen as a political move to reduce Lula’s rejection ahead of the 2026 election, with Minister Guilherme Boulos accusing Governor Tarcísio de Freitas of implementing the system to benefit concessionaires.

item.title: Lula sanciona lei que cria instituto federal em reduto de Motta
item.description: President Lula signed into law the creation of the Federal Institute of the Sertão Paraibano, headquartered in Patos, the political stronghold of Hugo Motta's family. The law also establishes 26,688 new federal government positions, with a budgetary impact of R$ 5.3 billion in 2026, and includes the delivery of 107 educational works across 16 states. The government-authored bill was approved via symbolic vote in Congress through an inter-party agreement.

item.title: Lula quer limite para juro do rotativo, diz Gleisi
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva has ordered the Central Bank and the Ministry of Finance to develop a study on alternatives to reduce interest rates on credit card revolving debt. The measure was announced by Minister of Institutional Relations Gleisi Hoffmann, who criticized the high charges applied to this form of lending.

item.title: Caiado diz que Flávio Bolsonaro não tem “vivência” para ser presidente
item.description: Governor of Goiás, Ronaldo Caiado (PSD), criticized Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a pre-candidate for president, stating he lacks the experience and lived practice to govern, particularly in dealing with Congress, the Supreme Court, and other governors. Caiado added that Flávio’s youth could lead to impulsive decisions that undermine the balance needed for governance through dialogue. Despite the criticism, Caiado pledged to grant broad and unrestricted amnesty to Jair Bolsonaro and those involved in the January 8 events if elected.

item.title: O que faria um eleitor de Flávio mudar o voto para Caiado
item.description: A Flávio Bolsonaro voter might shift support to Ronaldo Caiado if they perceive him as a more moderate, institutionalized alternative within the right wing capable of attracting votes without radicalizing the electorate. Political scientist Leonardo Barreto emphasizes that a significant segment of the Brazilian right — estimated at around 30% of the right-wing electorate — is not Bolsonarist and seeks to avoid burning bridges ahead of a potential runoff, favoring candidates who maintain electoral viability without alienating potential allies.

item.title: Ronaldo Caiado é o candidato à Presidência pelo PSD
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, former governor of Goiás, was announced as the PSD's presidential candidate in an event alongside Gilberto Kassab. In his speech, he promised a 'broad, general and unrestricted' amnesty and criticized Flávio Bolsonaro. Political scientist Leonardo Barreto analyzes the impact of this candidacy on the Brazilian right.

item.title: União oficializa ACM Neto como pré-candidato ao governo da Bahia
item.description: União Brasil officially launched former Salvador mayor ACM Neto as its pre-candidate for governor of Bahia in an event held in Feira de Santana. The ticket includes Zé Coca, current mayor of Jequié (PP), as the running mate for vice-governor. Despite his loss in the 2022 elections, the party is betting on Neto’s renewed candidacy to challenge for control of the Ondina Palace.

item.title: Capitão Wagner fica à frente em disputa pelo Senado no CE
item.description: A RealTime BigData survey released on March 30, 2026, shows former federal deputy Capitão Wagner (União Brasil) leading in all five tested scenarios for the Senate race in Ceará. The poll interviewed 1,600 Ceará voters between March 27–28, 2026, with a 2-point margin of error and 95% confidence level. Registered with the TSE under protocol CE-07645/2026, the study was self-funded.

item.title: Fui o 1º governador a explorar e comercializar terras raras, diz Caiado
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, governor of Goiás and PSD presidential pre-candidate, claimed to be the first governor to explore and commercialize rare earths in Brazil, citing a memorandum of understanding signed with the U.S. in March 2026. The federal government views the initiative as unconstitutional, as it involves negotiating strategic resources with a foreign government — a federal exclusive competence — and is considering judicial action if the agreement progresses to concrete measures.

item.title: Viana diz que prefeitura foi quem definiu emendas para Lagoinha
item.description: Senator Carlos Viana (Podemos-MG) denied that the R$ 3.6 million transfers to Fundação Oásis, linked to the Lagoinha Church, were 'Pix amendments', stating they were made via the 'fundo a fundo' modality with technical analysis by the National Social Assistance Fund and oversight by municipal councils, with prefeituras deciding the executing entity. He said the STF minister's request for additional clarifications is merely a technical procedural step, not an acknowledgment of irregularity, and labeled the suspicions a 'political narrative'.

item.title: El gobernador de Goiás se lanza a la carrera presidencial de Brasil como tercera vía
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado officially launched his presidential bid as the PSD’s candidate, positioning himself as a third way between Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva and Senator Flávio Bolsonaro. At 73, he announced his first act in office would be a broad, general, and unrestricted amnesty — a clear signal of support for jailed former president Jair Bolsonaro, convicted of plotting a coup. His entry intensifies the race and highlights the struggle for influence within Brazil’s center-right.

item.title: Elmano e Ciro empatam em disputa pelo governo do CE, diz pesquisa
item.description: Elmano de Freitas (PT) and Ciro Gomes (PSDB) are tied in the race for governor of Ceará with 32% each in the first-round scenario. In a second-round scenario without Ciro, Elmano leads with 45%, followed by Roberto Cláudio (União Brasil) at 28%. The poll also shows 58% approval of Elmano’s governance, compared to 40% disapproval.

item.title: IA da CGU emitiu 35 mil alertas de risco em licitações federais
item.description: The CGU reported that its AI system ALICE issued 35,000 risk alerts in federal procurement between January 2023 and February 2026, during Lula’s government. Of these, 729 triggered audits, leading to 1,523 formal communications to officials. The CGU stresses the alerts are preventive, not proof of wrongdoing, and require technical validation to confirm irregularities.

item.title: Feminicídio mostra que falhamos em educar “macho”, diz Lula
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that femicide reveals a failure in educating boys, describing the 'macho' as violent and believing they own the truth, and emphasized that education must teach boys they are not superior or owners of women. He made the remarks during the delivery of 107 educational works across 16 states, stressing that quality education depends on student-teacher interaction, not just financial investment. The government also released data on school connectivity and infrastructure, reinforcing its commitment to improving public education.

item.title: Como Avenida Brasil fez Dilma Rousseff mudar agenda em 2012
item.description: In October 2012, the final episode of the telenovela Avenida Brasil generated such national impact that President Dilma Rousseff adjusted her official schedule to avoid conflicting with its broadcast. She postponed a rally in São Paulo where she was set to appear alongside Fernando Haddad, the PT’s candidate for mayor, during the 2012 municipal elections campaign. This episode illustrates the significant cultural influence of television on political agendas in Brazil.

item.title: Lindbergh pede a Moraes que Bolsonaro volte para a Papudinha
item.description: Federal Deputy Lindbergh Farias (PT-RJ) urged Minister Alexandre de Moraes to revoke Jair Bolsonaro's house arrest and return him to closed regime at Papudinha, citing violations of precautionary measures after a video by Eduardo Bolsonaro at CPAC in the U.S. Bolsonaro's defense denies knowledge of the recording, while he remains under house arrest in Brasília recovering from bronchopneumonia. The petition notes the original ruling mandated immediate revocation upon any breach of conditions.

item.title: Valdemar elogia governos Lula com Alencar e critica gestão atual
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, national president of the PL, criticized the current Lula administration, stating that 'everything is going wrong' and highlighting the excessive number of 40 ministries as 'a barbarity'. He praised, however, Lula's first two terms (2003-2010) when José Alencar was vice-president, calling them a 'good government' with 'different investments'. Valdemar also argued that the current government must 'cut spending' and cited Bolsonaro's pandemic-era fund transfers to municipalities as an example of effective management, claiming Bolsonaro 'would have made a much better government' without the health crisis.

item.title: Lula sanciona lei que cria 17,8 mil cargos e gasto em 2026 chega a R$ 5,3 bilhões
item.description: President Lula signed a law creating 17,800 public sector positions, with 16,300 allocated to the Ministry of Education and 1,500 to the Ministry of Management and Innovation. The projected cost for 2026 is R$ 5.3 billion. The measure aims to strengthen state presence in key areas such as education and public administration.

item.title: Saiba o que Caiado diz sobre escala 6 X 1, Bolsa Família e reformas
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, governor of Goiás and PSD pre-candidate for president in 2026, advocated in a Poder360 interview (Jan 21, 2026) for an administrative reform based on meritocracy and performance evaluations, criticized the fiscal framework as a 'farce' and the tax reform for risking income concentration in wealthier states. Regarding Bolsa Família, he said he would maintain it but make it temporary, tied to professional qualification and labor market insertion. On the 6x1 work scale, he stated it no longer exists in practice and that the focus should be on increasing worker income through productivity, not just reducing hours.

item.title: Escolhido para ser candidato do PSD, Caiado tenta emplacar terceira via
item.description: Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado has been announced as the PSD's presidential candidate for the 2026 elections, aiming to position himself as a third way to break Brazil's political polarization. His candidacy seeks to offer an alternative to the two dominant ideological poles shaping the national electoral landscape.

item.title: PGR se manifesta contra afastamento de governador do Maranhão em ação movida pelo PCdoB
item.description: The PGR opposed the removal of Maranhão Governor Carlos Brandão, stating there is insufficient evidence he violated STF rulings on nepotism, though it acknowledged the PCdoB's claims are serious and warrant investigation. The case stems from a 2024 Solidariedade action alleging 14 individuals tied to the governor were appointed to non-political public roles, a matter already ruled on by Justice Alexandre de Moraes, who ordered their dismissal. Subprosecutor Claudia Sampaio Marques emphasized that removing a governor requires incontrovertible proof and warned against using judicial proceedings as a political battleground.

item.title: Defesa de Bolsonaro nega uso de celular em prisão domiciliar
item.description: The defense of Jair Bolsonaro denied that he violated the ban on cellphone use during house arrest, following claims that his son Eduardo Bolsonaro had filmed a video for him at a U.S. event. The Supreme Court requested explanations, as breaching house arrest conditions could result in his return to prison. Former First Lady Michelle Bolsonaro also denied receiving any such material.

item.title: Com Caiado candidato, a direita despe a fantasia de ‘centro’ e tende a fechar com Flávio
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado’s 2026 presidential candidacy exposes the absence of a true center in Brazilian politics, revealing that the right is consolidating around figures aligned with bolsonarism, like himself. Though presented as a moderate option by Gilberto Kassab, Caiado is clearly right-wing and has a history of supporting Bolsonaro, making his candidacy an attempt to fake centrism that does not exist. His low polling and trajectory suggest he may eventually endorse Flávio Bolsonaro, abandoning the façade of independence.

item.title: Tarcísio diz que nova ideia de subvenção do diesel é ‘razoável’ e SP deve aderir à proposta
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas stated that the federal government's proposal to grant a R$1.20 per liter subsidy on imported diesel is 'reasonable' and that his state should adhere to the measure, although it is still under discussion. He noted the cost would be split equally between the Union (R$0.60) and the states (R$0.60), and argued the proposal is viable because the federal government can offset revenue losses through other fiscal tools, unlike states that rely on ICMS and cannot forgo this revenue without breaching the Fiscal Responsibility Law.

item.title: “Caiado é um conservador legítimo”, diz Otoni de Paula
item.description: Federal deputy Otoni de Paula, who left the MDB to join the PSD, says his goal is to help former Goiás governor Ronaldo Caiado, a 2026 presidential pre-candidate, connect with the evangelical community. Otoni claims he previously brought President Lula (PT) closer to evangelicals and describes Caiado as a 'legitimate conservative'.

item.title: PGR pede que Moraes rejeite afastamento de governador desafeto de Dino no Maranhão
item.description: The PGR urged Justice Alexandre de Moraes to reject a request to remove Maranhão Governor Carlos Brandão, citing lack of incontestable proof that he violated STF orders on nepotism. Brandão’s defense denies wrongdoing and accuses Flávio Dino’s political group of exploiting the process for political-electoral gains, while Dino’s allies claim the governor failed to comply with orders to suspend three aides over nepotism. The case unfolds amid political and legal battles in Maranhão, ahead of the deadline for officials to step down before running in the 2026 elections.

item.title: PSD bate o martelo e escolhe Ronaldo Caiado como pré-candidato à presidência
item.description: The PSD has officially nominated Ronaldo Caiado, governor of Goiás, as its presidential pre-candidate for 2026, following the withdrawal of Ratinho Junior. Caiado pledged to pacify the country through a broad amnesty for those involved in the January 8 attacks, including former president Jair Bolsonaro. While Ratinho Junior praised the choice, Eduardo Leite of Rio Grande do Sul criticized it for deepening national polarization.

item.title: Malafaia cita avião 'bem velho' e diz que está 'orando a Deus' para que toque pessoas: 'Preciso de um mais novo'
item.description: Pastor Silas Malafaia defended replacing his 1985 aircraft, calling it a 'work tool' and saying he is praying to God for a newer one to 'reach people', in response to criticism over church resource use. He confirmed an informal meeting with Senator Flávio Bolsonaro in São Paulo, with no explicit electoral support negotiation, reiterating he sees figures like Tarcísio de Freitas and Michelle Bolsonaro as more competitive for 2026, but leaves open the possibility of supporting whoever consolidates in the conservative field.

item.title: Lula sanciona lei que estrutura carreiras e reajusta salários de servidores; impacto será de R$ 5,3 bi
item.description: President Lula signed a law restructuring federal public service careers and adjusting salaries, with an estimated budgetary impact of R$ 5.3 billion. The measure affects 200,000 active and retired servants, creating positions in education, health, justice, and management, and updating the Efficiency and Productivity Bonus for retirees. Part of the impact depends on the implementation of institutes and competitive exams, and may not be fully executed in 2026.

item.title: Lula nomeia Carlos Fávaro de volta para Ministério da Agricultura
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva has reappointed Carlos Fávaro as Minister of Agriculture and Livestock, as published in an extra edition of the Official Gazette of the Union on Monday, the 30th. This appointment reinforces the continuity of the federal government's agricultural policy.

item.title: Promessa de anistia a Bolsonaro na estreia de Caiado incomoda campanha de Flávio por possível impacto na base
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado’s promise of amnesty in his campaign launch irritated Flávio Bolsonaro’s team, as it signals a direct bid for the same Bolsonarist electorate. Despite the discomfort, Flávio’s allies argue for maintaining a strategy focused on economic agenda and alliances, avoiding a narrative clash for now. The prevailing view is that Caiado may act as a 'side line' — taking on the ideological fight against Lula — thereby allowing Flávio to pursue a more calibrated approach.

item.title: Erika Hilton pede que AGU adote medidas contra Flávio por ataques a processo eleitoral
item.description: Federal deputy Erika Hilton (PSOL) has urged the AGU to take action against Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) for undermining the legitimacy of the 2022 Brazilian election by claiming foreign interference without evidence. She argues this constitutes an abuse of parliamentary mandate, spreads disinformation threatening democracy, and demands accountability, retraction, and platform intervention.
